Drought foreshadows famine; excessive rain, famine and
threat of an attack by a foreign foe; untimely rain, disease; and a
cloudless rain, the king's death.
Change of weather conditions in the hot and cold seasons, and
the improper functioning, of the seasons foreshadow danger to the
kingdom, fear of diseases and divine visitations after six months.
[The commentator takes as an adjective of Thm, which
would mean—threat of diseases arising from past misdeeds.]

monsoon forebodes the death of the chief monarch. Should there be
